examples: Volcanic ash showered down on the town after the eruption.
Etna's eruption history dates back as far as 1500 B.C.
Suppose Mount Etna erupted everyday, or imagine that each eruption there kills thousands of people.
The eruption also engulfed a small Maori village, Te Wairoa, in mud, ash and rock.
Its last eruption was in 1994, and traces of the black lava can still be seen.
